events.title: US and Iran Nearing Peace Deal, Timing Disputed
events.desc: US President Donald Trump announced that a deal to end the war with Iran was scheduled for signing on Sunday, June 15th, and that the Strait of Hormuz would be immediately opened. However, Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson, Esmaeil Baghaei, stated that the exact timing of the signing of the Islamabad memorandum was not set for Sunday and expressed caution due to hesitation from the other side. Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif indicated that a final text had been reached and an electronic signing was expected within 24 hours.

events.title: Israeli Strikes in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Lebanon reported Israeli strikes in the country's south on Saturday, with the Israeli army issuing evacuation warnings for Nabatieh and over 20 other locations. The state-run National News Agency (NNA) stated that Israeli airstrikes had hit several areas, particularly around the southern city of Nabatieh. These strikes occurred as the US and Iran indicated they were close to reaching a deal on ending the Middle East war that could also include Lebanon.

events.title: Two Killed in Israeli Strike on Gaza
events.desc: An Israeli airstrike on southern Gaza resulted in the deaths of two individuals and injured another, according to Palestinian authorities. This incident occurred amidst heightened tensions in the region.

events.title: Three Indian Sailors Killed in US Strike on Oil Tanker
events.desc: Three Indian sailors were killed when the Palau-flagged oil tanker they were on was struck by a missile fired from a US fighter jet. The US military had attacked the vessel after accusing it of not complying with directions. The surviving crew were rescued off the coast of Oman.

events.title: Israeli Strikes Hit Lebanese City of Tyre
events.desc: Israel conducted air strikes on the Lebanese city of Tyre, which it stated were ordered "in response to Hezbollah's firing at Israeli territory." This action followed an Iranian warning to Israel to cease attacks on its Lebanese ally, Hezbollah. The strikes targeted areas in southern Lebanon.

events.title: Iran and Israel Pause Strikes, Warn of Retaliation
events.desc: Both Iran and Israel announced a pause in hostilities, with Israel's Prime Minister stating the country was holding fire "at the moment." Iran's armed forces also confirmed they had stopped military action. However, both sides issued warnings of retaliation should the ceasefire be breached again.

events.title: Israel Strikes Beirut Suburb Days After US-Brokered Truce
events.desc: Israel carried out strikes on a Beirut suburb, days after a US-brokered truce. The Israeli military stated the attacks were ordered "in response to Hezbollah's firing at Israeli territory," indicating a continued escalation despite efforts at de-escalation.

events.title: One Dead, Five Injured in Shooting in Israel
events.desc: A shooting incident occurred in Israel where the suspect opened fire from a car in several locations, resulting in one death and five injuries. The suspect was later shot dead by security forces.

events.title: Israeli Air Strikes Kill 17 in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Reports indicate that Israeli air strikes in southern Lebanon have resulted in the deaths of 17 people. Nine of the fatalities occurred in a series of attacks in the town of Tayr Debba, according to Lebanon's state news agency.

events.title: Funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei Announced
events.desc: Iran's state media reported that the funeral for the late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, who was killed in Israeli and US strikes in February, will begin in Tehran on July 4th, with burial scheduled for July 9th in Mashhad.

events.title: Israel and Iran Trade Strikes
events.desc: Israel and Iran engaged in direct strikes against each other on June 7th, escalating tensions in the region. This exchange occurred amid broader regional conflicts and ongoing negotiations for a ceasefire.

events.title: Strikes on Iranian Police Infrastructure
events.desc: At least 80 police stations or infrastructure related to law enforcement agencies and the Basij paramilitary group have been damaged or destroyed in Iran during the first three weeks of the conflict with the United States and Israel. Experts suggest the aim is to degrade the Iranian regime's "repressive capacity."

events.title: Russian Cluster Munitions Found in Mali
events.desc: Unexploded Russian-made cluster munition bomblets and damage consistent with their impact were discovered in a village in northern Mali, despite Mali being a state party to the convention banning such weapons. This finding occurred after the Malian military announced airstrikes.

events.title: Demolitions Across Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Satellite imagery indicates that at least 46 out of 54 towns and villages within the Israel Defense Forces' "Yellow Line" in southern Lebanon have sustained heavy damage, with some being entirely flattened. Much of this destruction and demolition has occurred since a fragile ceasefire was agreed upon.

events.title: Landslides at DRC Mines Under M23 Control
events.desc: Since the start of 2026, at least four landslides at the Rubaya mines in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), a major source of coltan, have reportedly killed hundreds of people. The mines are currently under the control of the Rwandan-backed M23 group.
